action.hypothesis: Round 45 showed that the strengthened `gated` recipe (`signum`, `LR=2.0e-3`, `USE_HADAMARD=1`) transfers from `K=128` down to `K=96`, improving the prior `gated@96` result while keeping a smooth, still-improving curve. The next single question is whether that gain carries one more step down the cost ladder: if we hold the improved recipe fixed and reduce only `K` to `64`, can `gated` produce a clearly better lower-cost point than the old `K=64` result, or does the Hadamard benefit fade before this support level?
action.summary: Continue the active `gated` mainline and keep the improved recipe fixed: `ARCHITECTURE=gated`, `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, `LR=2.0e-3`, `AUXK_ALPHA=0.03125`, and `USE_HADAMARD=1`. Change only `K` from `96` to `64` to test whether the new recipe yields a stronger lower-cost deployment point at the next allowed support level. Judge the run by the full trajectory as well as the endpoint: if it stays smooth and finishes materially below the old `gated@64` control (`0.195828`), the improved recipe is genuinely useful beyond the `K=128` anchor; if it falls back toward the old curve, treat the current gain as mainly a high-K effect.
action.self_review: This stays on the current mainline family, respects the fixed `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12` rule, and changes exactly one primary variable: `K`. It follows the prescribed order because the clean baseline, LR, optimizer, loss, and preprocess questions have already been answered on this recipe, and Round 45 provided positive evidence that the improved recipe transfers at least to `K=96`. The main risk is that `K=64` may sit past the regime where Hadamard helps, but that is the highest-value remaining single-question probe for LUTurbo-relevant cost reduction.
notes_to_memory: Rounds 43-45 established a stronger `gated` mainline recipe: `signum`, `LR=2.0e-3`, `AUXK_ALPHA=0.03125`, and `USE_HADAMARD=1`, with reproducible improvement at `K=128` and successful transfer to `K=96`.
Round 46 isolates the next deployment-relevant K question by testing whether that improved recipe also lowers `gated@64` enough to strengthen the lower-cost part of the frontier.
next_hypotheses: If improved `gated@64` clearly beats the old `K=64` result with a healthy curve, continue once more to `K=32` under the same recipe to locate where the transfer breaks.
If `K=64` regresses toward the old control or shows weaker curve quality, stop the downward sweep and treat the improved recipe mainly as a `K=96/128` high-quality lane.

result.description: Continue the active `gated` mainline and keep the improved recipe fixed: `ARCHITECTURE=gated`, `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, `LR=2.0e-3`, `AUXK_ALPHA=0.03125`, and `USE_HADAMARD=1`. Change only `K` from `96` to `64` to test whether the new recipe yields a stronger lower-cost deployment point at the next allowed support level. Judge the run by the full trajectory as well as the endpoint: if it stays smooth and finishes materially below the old `gated@64` control (`0.195828`), the improved recipe is genuinely useful beyond the `K=128` anchor; if it falls back toward the old curve, treat the current gain as mainly a high-K effect.

It follows the prescribed order because the clean baseline, LR, optimizer, loss, and preprocess questions have already been answered on this recipe, and Round 45 provided positive evidence that the improved recipe transfers at least to `K=96`. The main risk is that `K=64` may sit past the regime where Hadamard helps, but that is the highest-value remaining single-question probe for LUTurbo-relevant cost reduction.
